subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The James Webb Space Telescope
description: A 60-second lesson on The James Webb Space Telescope.

hook.text: JWST is the most powerful space telescope ever built. It sees infrared light from the earliest galaxies in the universe.

buildup.text: Its gold-coated mirror is 6.5 meters wide, folded to fit inside a rocket. It unfolded perfectly in space.

discovery.text: Launched in December 2021, its first images revealed galaxies from just 300 million years after the Big Bang.

twist.text: JWST cost $10 billion and took 25 years to build. It was nearly canceled multiple times before launch.

climax.text: It orbits 1.5 million km from Earth at the L2 Lagrange point, shielded from the Sun by a tennis-court-sized sunshade.

punchline.text: Twenty-five years of patience. Now seeing the universe's first light.

quiz.question: Where does the James Webb Space Telescope orbit?
options: At the L2 Lagrange point, 1.5 million km from Earth
In low Earth orbit, alongside the ISS
Around the Moon in a polar orbit
